toolchain.verilator: >= 5.048 required (chipyard's 5.022 cannot compile difftest: waveform.h uses VerilatedTraceBaseC)

why_this_exists: This is the XiangShan variant that actually simulates. The XSTop freeze in xiangshan/ is the clean FPGA-platform SoC top but does not retire instructions from a bare AXI testbench; SimTop is XiangShan's own simulation top (core + difftest + simulated MMIO/flash) and boots real workloads.

verification.status: PASS — emu rebuilt from THIS frozen RTL (zero Chisel elaboration) boots CoreMark
evidence: chisel_runMain_invocations=0  (build used the frozen Verilog, not a fresh elaboration)
coremark-2-iteration: HIT GOOD TRAP @0x80001ca0, 663,691 instrs, cycleCnt 505,128, IPC 1.313907, 469 iters/sec
identical to the from-source build => the frozen RTL is faithful
difftest enabled: every retired instruction checked against NEMU
verification.reproduce: sim/xs_simtop_verify.pbs (copies rtl/ into XiangShan build/rtl, touches SimTop.sv so make skips Chisel, then `make emu`)

contents.filelist.f: generator filelist (incomplete, as with XSTop — compile all of rtl/)
contents.DifftestMacros.svh: difftest macro header emitted into build/generated-src
contents.meta/git-provenance.txt: git log/diff header emitted by the XiangShan Makefile

notes: Running this needs XiangShan's difftest C++ harness and a NEMU reference (ready-to-run/riscv64-nemu-interpreter-so); the RTL alone is not standalone-runnable because it imports DPI functions (xs_assert_v2, DifftestFlash, ...). Use sim/xs_simtop_verify.pbs.
No flat/ file: the flat SimTop cannot lint standalone anyway (DPI imports are resolved by difftest's C++), so rtl/ + filelist is the usable form.
SRAMs are behavioral models. Fine for simulation/formal, not PDK synthesis.
SimTop carries difftest instrumentation; for a clean, instrumentation-free SoC top use xiangshan/ (XSTop).
